s: eten & drinken means 'food & drinks' in Dutch
q: What does 'eten & drinken' mean in English?
e: Food and beverages; essential vocabulary for Dutch dining culture.

q: What does "de groente" mean in English?
e: "de groente" means "the vegetables". Dutch vegetables are famous for their sweetness; Dutch tomatoes won the 2003 Taste of Summer competition

q: What does "het fruit" mean in English?
e: "het fruit" means "the fruits". Netherlands exports millions of tons of fruit annually despite their northern climate

q: What does "het brood" mean in English?
e: "het brood" means "the bread". Dutch bread has a crust so prized that bakeries compete for "best crust" awards

q: What does "de boterham" mean in English?
e: "de boterham" means "the slice of bread". The Dutch eat 4 boterham sandwiches per person per week on average

q: What does "de kaas" mean in English?
e: "de kaas" means "the cheese". Netherlands produces over 400,000 tons of cheese annually and is the world's largest exporter

q: What does "de hagelslag" mean in English?
e: "de hagelslag" means "the chocolate sprinkles". Hagelslag is uniquely Dutch—kids have been eating this chocolate breakfast topping for over 100 years

q: What does "de pindakaas" mean in English?
e: "de pindakaas" means "the peanut butter". While Dutch claim hagelslag as uniquely theirs, peanut butter sandwiches became trendy in Netherlands post-WWII

q: What does "de melk" mean in English?
e: "de melk" means "the milk". Dutch milk comes from some of the world's happiest cows, grazing year-round in flat green meadows

q: What does "de koffie" mean in English?
e: "de koffie" means "the coffee". Netherlands is the world's largest coffee trader despite not growing any coffee themselves

q: What does "de thee" mean in English?
e: "de thee" means "the tea". High tea culture in Netherlands reflects centuries of trade with Asian spice routes

q: What does "de aardappel" mean in English?
e: "de aardappel" means "the potato". Potatoes arrived in Netherlands from South America and became a dietary staple by 1700s

q: What does "de aardappeleter" mean in English?
e: "de aardappeleter" means "the potato-eater". The term "aardappeleter" was used by Dutch nobility to mock farmers who ate potatoes

q: What does "het bier" mean in English?
e: "het bier" means "the beer". Dutch beer exports exceed 2 billion euros annually; Heineken was founded in Amsterdam in 1864

q: What does "de wijn" mean in English?
e: "de wijn" means "the wine". Wine was a luxury import; Dutch merchants became famous for wine trading along European rivers

q: What does "het water" mean in English?
e: "het water" means "the water". Dutch water quality is among the safest in the world; tap water is better than bottled in Netherlands

q: What does "het vlees" mean in English?
e: "het vlees" means "the meat". Dutch meat industry has global influence; Dutch beef and pork reach 80+ countries

q: What does "de kip" mean in English?
e: "de kip" means "the chicken". Chicken meat consumption in Netherlands jumped from 7kg to 15kg per person since 1990

q: What does "de friet" mean in English?
e: "de friet" means "the French fries". Belgian fries vs Dutch fries is a famous culinary debate; Dutch fries use horse meat mayo

q: What does "frietje met" mean in English?
e: "frietje met" means "French fries with mayonnaise". "Frietje met" is specifically Dutch—mayo on fries became iconic Dutch street food

q: What does "de frikandel" mean in English?
e: "de frikandel" means "Dutch meat sausage". Frikandel is a beloved Dutch street snack with mysterious meat filling that fuels late-night conversations

q: What does "de kroket" mean in English?
e: "de kroket" means "Dutch fried ragout bar". Croquettes arrived in Netherlands via Spanish cuisine; Dutch version filled with ragout is signature

q: What does "de pannenkoek" mean in English?
e: "de pannenkoek" means "the pancake". Dutch pancakes are thinner than American pancakes and can be sweet or savory

q: What does "de stroopwafel" mean in English?
e: "de stroopwafel" means "the syrup waffle". Stroopwafel was invented in Gouda, Netherlands in the 1800s and is now sold at train stations worldwide

q: What does "drop" mean in English?
e: "drop" means "liquorice". Dutch licorice (drop) is polarizing: people either love it or hate it—there's no middle ground

s: Translate the Dutch word "de, het snoep"
q: What does "de, het snoep" mean in English?
e: "de, het snoep" means "the sweets". Dutch children receive "snoepgeld" (candy money) as pocket money since the 1950s

q: What does "de straat" mean in English?
e: "de straat" means "the street". Dutch streets are world-famous for cycling infrastructure and narrow medieval layouts in city centers

q: What does "de weg" mean in English?
e: "de weg" means "the road". Netherlands has more miles of bike paths than car roads, unique in the world

q: What does "de snelweg" mean in English?
e: "de snelweg" means "the highway". Dutch highways use dynamic speed limits that adjust based on traffic and air quality

q: What does "de auto" mean in English?
e: "de auto" means "the car". Netherlands has one of the lowest car ownership rates in Europe due to excellent public transit

q: What does "de bus" mean in English?
e: "de bus" means "the bus". Dutch public buses run with precision; they're 90%+ on-time across the country

q: What does "de trein" mean in English?
e: "de trein" means "the train". Dutch trains are electric and the Nederlandse Spoorwegen (NS) operates one of Europe's busiest networks

q: What does "de tram" mean in English?
e: "de tram" means "the tram". Amsterdam trams are iconic; the tram system dates back to 1875

q: What does "de halte" mean in English?
e: "de halte" means "the stopping place". Bus stops in Netherlands have real-time digital displays showing exact arrival times

q: What does "de metro" mean in English?
e: "de metro" means "the metro". Amsterdam metro opened in 1977 and was built partially below sea level

q: What does "de boot" mean in English?
e: "de boot" means "the boat". Dutch boat culture is ancient; Holland's golden age was built on maritime trade

q: What does "het vliegtuig" mean in English?
e: "het vliegtuig" means "the airplane". Amsterdam airport (Schiphol) is one of Europe's busiest, built 2 meters BELOW sea level

q: What does "het vliegveld" mean in English?
e: "het vliegveld" means "the airport". Schiphol airport pioneered the "hub and spoke" model for international air travel

q: What does "de haven" mean in English?
e: "de haven" means "the port". Rotterdam is Europe's largest port by cargo tonnage; it handles 400+ million tons annually

q: What does "het station" mean in English?
e: "het station" means "the railway station". Amsterdam Central Station is built on three artificial islands sunk into the canals

q: What does "het kantoor" mean in English?
e: "het kantoor" means "the office". Netherlands has the world's highest percentage of remote-work friendly office culture

q: What does "de winkel" mean in English?
e: "de winkel" means "the shop". Dutch shops close early (5-6pm) compared to other European countries—efficiency first

q: What does "de supermarkt" mean in English?
e: "de supermarkt" means "the supermarket". Albert Heijn (AH) supermarkets started in Netherlands and are iconic to Dutch shopping

q: What does "het ziekenhuis" mean in English?
e: "het ziekenhuis" means "the hospital". Dutch healthcare system consistently ranks #2 globally for quality and accessibility

q: What does "de apotheek" mean in English?
e: "de apotheek" means "the pharmacy". Dutch pharmacies are highly regulated; pharmacists have 5+ years of training

q: What does "het zwembad" mean in English?
e: "het zwembad" means "the swimming pool". Swimming proficiency is required education in Netherlands due to flat, water-heavy landscape

q: What does "de sporthal" mean in English?
e: "de sporthal" means "the sports hall". Dutch sports halls host volleyball, badminton, and basketball as popular team sports

q: What does "het politiebureau" mean in English?
e: "het politiebureau" means "the police station". Dutch police are known for restraint; handgun use is rare and heavily scrutinized

q: What does "de bibliotheek" mean in English?
e: "de bibliotheek" means "the library". Dutch public libraries are free and highly used; over 35 million visits annually

q: What does "de school" mean in English?
e: "de school" means "the school". Dutch schools emphasize cycling safety from kindergarten, preparing kids for life on bikes
